# Solarferm

> Fuel for the bioeconomy. Solarferm produces fermentation-grade sugar from carbon
> dioxide, hydrogen, and existing energy infrastructure — an abundant, predictable,
> agriculture-independent feedstock for biomanufacturing at industrial scale.

## What Solarferm does
Solarferm makes industrial sugar, the critical feedstock for biomanufacturing, from
carbon dioxide and energy rather than from crops. The process combines proven
thermocatalytic chemistry with electrochemical bioreactor technology in a continuous
production loop, turning energy and CO₂ into programmable sugars (commodity, specialty,
niche, and rare). Output is decoupled from land, weather, harvest, and agricultural
commodity volatility, with consistent quality and predictable pricing.

## Why it matters
Biomanufacturing is becoming national infrastructure, viewed through the lens of food
security, supply chain resilience, and sovereign capability. Its binding constraint is
feedstock supply. Next-generation biomanufacturing needs feedstocks that are abundant,
predictable, cost-effective, and decoupled from agricultural volatility — the constraint
Solarferm exists to remove.

## Who it serves
- Energy companies: an energy-to-product outlet that turns energy and CO₂ into a
  storable, sellable product and diversifies revenue for the energy transition.
- Biomanufacturers: fermentation-grade sugar with pricing decoupled from crops, delivered
  continuously from modular plants sited near fermentation capacity.
- Ingredient makers: programmable sugars, from commodity to niche and rare, with lower
  carbon intensity than agricultural sources.

## Key points
- Inputs: carbon dioxide (CO₂) and hydrogen (H₂), plus energy.
- Energy-source agnostic: deployable on existing infrastructure today, scaling onto
  next-generation renewables as they reach parity.
- Secure, onshore, resilient: supply independent of farms, weather, and distant
  commodity markets.
- Based in New Zealand.

## Contact
- Email: hello@solarferm.com
- Website: https://solarferm.com/
